expectations.text: States neither modality is universally superior for hypertrophy
expectations.evidence: Actually claims 'Free weights are superior for hypertrophy in most contexts.' This contradicts the expected answer that neither is universally superior.

expectations.text: Notes machines are useful for variation, reducing technical constraints, and targeting volume
expectations.evidence: Section on 'When Machines Are Useful' covers accessory work, joint management, beginners, and deload phases.

expectations.text: Recommends keeping a base of compound free-weight work
expectations.evidence: States: 'Anchor with free weights. Make barbell bench your primary pressing movement.'

expectations.text: Practical takeaway: use both, pick the tool that best fits the muscle/movement/fatigue profile
expectations.evidence: Recommends using machines as accessories after free-weight anchors. Lists specific use cases for each.
